answers: Etiquette is a set of social rules that guide polite and respectful behavior. Good manners make interactions smoother and leave positive impressions on others.
Basic manners include saying please and thank you, greeting people warmly, being punctual, listening when others speak, and respecting personal space. These small things matter a lot.

Professional etiquette includes being on time, dressing appropriately, using proper email greetings, maintaining eye contact in meetings, and following up on commitments. First impressions are important.

Dining etiquette basics: napkin on lap, chew with mouth closed, don't talk with food in your mouth, wait for everyone to be served before eating, and thank the host. These show respect and consideration.
